channel.title: Wasteland on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.
channel.description: Recent content in Wasteland on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.

item.title: Fallout And Wasteland
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Fallout and Wasteland.

Not Wasteland 2 or three, I’m talking about the original 1988 Wasteland. The reason I want to talk about this is I see a lot of confusing comments online about the influence that Wasteland had on Fallout. Some people think it was a direct influence. Some people think it was a really strong influence. Some people say I said it was an influence, so let me talk about that. I did briefly touch on this in a video I did on a link it in the description on Fallout influences and very clearly in there I say Wasteland was an influence, but I also say it wasn’t strongly an influence. Um, so let me go into more detail about that and right now I can just say, if people go, was wayon an influence on Fallout?

item.title: How I Almost Made Wasteland 2
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about how I almost made Wasteland 2 in the late 90s, something I mentioned when I was having my chat with Scott Campbell kind of mentioned.

Oh yeah, I almost. You know Interplay wasn’t going to get that license and yeah, let me, let me go into detail. So let me give context.

Um, so we talked about how some people on the team had played Wasteland. I haven’t seen of the core team. Probably most of them had played Wasteland, but most of the people who played Wasteland had never finished it. They played it, saw how it worked. It was hard to play those games. Um, early dos games.
